title: ReviewCheck MCP tool contracts
description: Tool contracts for the local MCP server (an add-on running inside the host agent). Reference: docs/13. CONSTRAINTS: (1) every tool that presents a block ALWAYS returns 'code' and 'explanation' together (co-presence, docs/13 §1.1); (2) the explanation is anchored to line citations (grounding, docs/13 §1 2-bis); (3) the review outcome is the SUM of the per-block human decisions (accept/request_correction) + an explicit confirmation: the AI neither judges nor approves (human-in-the-loop, docs/13 §1.2). No backend/DB: the 'session' is a handle to a LOCAL state file (see session-state.schema.json).

Block.description: A unit of intent. code and explanation are both REQUIRED (co-presence). The code is the source of truth; the explanation is anchored to the code (grounding).

code.description: The block's code (diff + minimal context). ALWAYS present. Source of truth.

explanation.description: An explanation that DESCRIBES AND ASKS, never a verdict. Anchored to the code (grounding).

link.description: Edges to related blocks (from the graph's structural facts, not invented)

citations.description: The code lines the explanation is anchored to (grounding).

uncertainty.description: Where the model is uncertain / anchoring is weak; null if absent.

related_block_ids.description: Edges (F3): related blocks, peekable on demand.

tools.name: get_review_plan
tools.description: Reads a set of changes and returns the plan: blocks, reading order, interaction points (seams), and the FIRST complete block (code + explanation). Opens a local session. SOURCE: Mode A (PRIMARY) = LOCAL pre-PR diff (git, no token/network): review what the agent/you just wrote before opening the PR. Mode B (secondary) = a PR on the platform (via local token).

ref.description: 'working' (uncommitted changes, default) | 'staged' | a git range (e.g. 'origin/main...HEAD') | a commit.

session.description: Handle to the local session (state file).

interaction_points.description: Seams to verify (F13), derived from the graph — attention pointers, not verdicts.

tools.name: next_block
tools.description: Advances to the next recommended block and returns it COMPLETE (code + explanation together). The client must print code and explanation together, never on a separate request.

tools.name: get_block
tools.description: Returns a specific block COMPLETE (code + explanation together). Also used to 'peek' at a related block without advancing (F4).

tools.name: accept_block
tools.description: The user ACCEPTS the block (they understood it and take responsibility for it). A human decision recorded in the local state file.

tools.name: request_correction
tools.description: The user REQUESTS A CORRECTION on the block, with a note. Marks the block as 'to correct' and stores the note (it becomes a comment in the request-changes).

tools.name: review_status
tools.description: Session status: position, progress, accepted blocks, and blocks to correct.

tools.name: submit_review
tools.description: Closes the review. The outcome is the SUM of the per-block human decisions (never an AI verdict). Fails if any block is undecided. Behavior depends on the SOURCE: (A) LOCAL (pre-PR): posts NOTHING — returns a summary + a list of corrections to apply; outcome 'ready_to_proceed' (all accepted) or 'corrections_to_apply' (>=1 correction). Here the self-approval problem does not exist. (B) PULL REQUEST: posts the outcome to the platform with 'confirm':true (without confirmation = preview); outcome 'approve' or 'request_changes'; FALLBACK 'comment_only' if the platform forbids self-reviewing your own PR.

confirm.description: Mode B only: must be true to post. In Mode A it is ignored (nothing is posted).

outcome.description: Mode A (local): ready_to_proceed | corrections_to_apply. Mode B (PR): approve | request_changes | comment_only (self-review fallback).

posted.description: true only in Mode B with confirm=true and after publishing. Always false in Mode A.

notes.description: The requested corrections: in Mode A they are an actionable to-do list; in Mode B they become comments.

undecided_blocks.description: Undecided blocks: if non-empty, submit does not proceed.

forbidden_tools.description: Tools that MUST NOT exist: the AI neither judges nor decides correctness. The outcome comes only from accept_block/request_correction (human) + an explicit confirmation in submit_review.
